NCT02475681.name: ELEVATE-TN
NCT02475681.group: Treatment-naive CLL aged >=65 or unfit, acalabrutinib monotherapy vs chlorambucil+obinutuzumab (median f/u 58 mo, IRC-assessed PFS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Investigator-assessed progression-free survival (primary, acalabrutinib vs chlorambucil+obinutuzumab, HR)

NCT02475681.snippet: Source: Sharman JP et al. Lancet 2020;395:1278-91 + Sharman 2022 Leukemia 58-mo update (ELEVATE-TN).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 535 adults with previously untreated CLL aged 65+ or 18-64 with comorbidities (CIRS >6 or CrCl 30-69 mL/min) randomized 1:1:1 open-label to acalabrutinib 100 mg PO BID monotherapy (n=179), acalabrutinib plus obinutuzumab (n=179), or chlorambucil plus obinutuzumab (n=177). Primary comparison for this app: acalabrutinib monotherapy vs chlorambucil+obinutuzumab.

evidence.label: Primary - PFS (Acala-mono vs Clb+Obi, 58-month update)
evidence.text: Investigator-assessed progression-free survival at median 58.2-month follow-up: acalabrutinib monotherapy median PFS not reached vs chlorambucil+obinutuzumab median 27.8 months; hazard ratio 0.21 (95% CI 0.14-0.32; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - ORR, OS, Subgroups
evidence.text: IRC-assessed ORR: acalabrutinib mono 90% vs Clb+Obi 79% (58-mo update). Overall survival trend favours acalabrutinib (HR 0.82, 95% CI 0.59-1.15; immature). Benefit consistent across del17p, TP53-mutated, IGHV-unmutated subgroups. Safety: less neutropenia and infusion reactions than Clb+Obi; atrial fibrillation 6%, bleeding 4% - lower than first-generation ibrutinib historical benchmarks.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by del17p and ECOG PS. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label allocation (active/chemo arms differ in route, mitigated by independent review committee for PFS). D3 LOW - 95% completion. D4 LOW - IRC-adjudicated PFS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria~1. NCT02970318.name: ASCEND
NCT02970318.group: Relapsed/refractory CLL, acalabrutinib vs investigators choice (idelalisib+rituximab or bendamustine+rituximab), IRC-assessed PFS HR (46-mo update)

allOutcomes.title: IRC-assessed progression-free survival (primary, acalabrutinib vs investigator choice, HR)

NCT02970318.snippet: Source: Ghia P et al. JCO 2020;38:2849-61 + Jurczak 2023 Am J Hematol 46-mo update (ASCEND).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 310 adults with relapsed/refractory CLL having received >=1 prior line randomized 1:1 open-label to acalabrutinib 100 mg PO BID (n=155) or investigator choice of idelalisib+rituximab or bendamustine+rituximab (n=155).

evidence.label: Primary - PFS (46-month update)
evidence.source: Jurczak 2023 Am J Hematol (46-mo update), Figure 2
evidence.text: IRC-assessed progression-free survival at median 46.5-month follow-up: acalabrutinib median PFS not reached vs investigator choice median 16.8 months; hazard ratio 0.28 (95% CI 0.20-0.38; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met with robust, durable effect.

evidence.label: Secondary - OS and Subgroups
evidence.text: Overall survival trend favours acalabrutinib (46-mo HR 0.82, 95% CI 0.59-1.15). Consistent PFS benefit across del17p, TP53-mutated, complex karyotype, and IGHV-unmutated subgroups. Safety: atrial fibrillation 8%, major haemorrhage 3%, hypertension 7%. Fewer treatment discontinuations due to AEs than idelalisib arm.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by del17p, ECOG PS, number of prior therapies. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (route/schedule differ between arms, mitigated by IRC). D3 LOW - 92% completion. D4 LOW - IRC-adjudicated PFS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Men and women ≥ 18 years of age.~2. title: RapidMeta Haematology | Acalabrutinib (Second-Generation BTKi) for CLL v1.1
